findings.title: Latent stored XSS: DB-sourced product id rendered as unescaped JSX child in a fragment applied via innerHTML
findings.description:
The @jiso/server JSX runtime (packages/server/src/jsx-runtime.ts) escapes attribute values via escapeAttribute (renderJsxAttributes, L66) but inserts text CHILDREN raw via renderJsxChildren -> String(children) (L79-84), an explicitly-documented 'open question'. In product-grid.tsx, renderProductCard renders `

{item.id}

` (L92-93) where item.id is DB data from loadProductGrid -> db.read('products'). The product grid is a fragmentTarget; its markup is delivered as a server fragment and applied on the client via innerHTML/insertAdjacentHTML (per CLAUDE.md threat model item 3 and runtime/response-fragment-apply.ts / morph.ts). Per the framework's own contract (CLAUDE.md: 'Server fragment renderers must run user/DB strings through escapeHtml/escapeAttribute ... an unescaped interpolation is stored XSS'), DB strings are untrusted for XSS. NOTE ON EXPLOITABILITY: in the current reference app this is NOT exploitable because product ids are server-seeded ('p1'/'p2'/'p3' in createCommerceDb, app.ts L270) and the only product write path (addToCart, app.ts L424) decrements stock on an already-looked-up existing product, so no code path lets an attacker introduce a product id containing '
findings.recommendation:
Do not rely on the data source being benign — encode at the sink. Either (a) make the @jiso/server JSX runtime escape text children (renderJsxChildren should HTML-escape String(children) for non-pre-rendered nodes, with an explicit raw/unsafe opt-in for composed pre-rendered HTML), closing the framework-wide gap; or (b) until that lands, wrap DB/user string interpolations in escapeHtml() inside fragment renderers, e.g. `

{escapeHtml(item.id)}

`. Regenerate the lowered IR (src/generated/product-grid.tsx) after the fix and add a test asserting a product id containing HTML metacharacters is escaped in the rendered fragment.
